content: During a welding operation, thermal power of 2500 W is incident normally on a metallic surface. As shown in the figure below (figure is NOT to scale), the heated area is circular. Out of the incident power, $85 \%$ of the power is absorbed within a circle of radius 5 mm while $65 \%$ is absorbed within an inner concentric circle of radius 3 mm . The power density in the shaded area is __________ $\mathrm{Wmm}^{-2}$ (rounded off to 2 decimal places).
explanation:
Total incident heat is $=2500 \mathrm{~W}$

5 m palte absorption $=85 \%$

5 m plate sbrosption $=65 \%$

Absorption by shaded area $=(85-65) \%=20 \%$

$$ \begin{aligned} q_{\text {absorbed (shaded) }} & =2500 \times 0.20=500 \mathrm{~W} \\ \text { Heat density } & =\frac{500}{A_{\text {shaded }}}=\frac{500}{\pi\left(5^2-3^2\right)}=9.947 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{mm}^2 \end{aligned} $$

content: Wien’s law is stated as follows: λmT = C, where C is 2898 μm.K and λm is the wavelength at which the emissive power of a black body is maximum for a given temperature T. The spectral hemispherical emissivity (ελ) of a surface is shown in the figure below (1 Å = 10-10 m). The temperature at which the total hemispherical emissivity will be highest is K (round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
Apply Wien's law :

$$ \begin{aligned} \because & \lambda_m T = C \\\\ \text{For} & (6000 \times 10^{10}) \times T_{\max } = 2898 \times 10^{-6} \\\\ \therefore & T_{\max} = 4830 \text{ K} \end{aligned} $$

Therefore, the temperature at which the total hemispherical emissivity will be highest is 4830 K.

content: A flat plate made of cast iron is exposed to a solar flux of 600 W/m2 at an ambient temperature of 25 °C. Assume that the entire solar flux is absorbed by the plate. Cast iron has a low-temperature absorptivity of 0.21. Use Stefan-Boltzmann constant = 5.669 × 10-8 W/m2-K4. Neglect all other modes of heat transfer except radiation. Under the aforementioned conditions, the radiation equilibrium temperature of the plate is __________ °C (round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
Given :

Solar flux, $Q_{\text{enter}} = 600 \, \text{W/m}^2$

Absorptivity, $\alpha = 0.21$

Ambient temperature, $T_{\infty} = 25^{\circ} \mathrm{C} = 298 \, \mathrm{K}$

Stefan Boltzmann constant, $\sigma = 5.669 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W/m}^2\mathrm{K}^4$

Since the entire flux is absorbed by the plate, we can consider the absorptivity as 1.

According to Kirchhoff's law :

Emissivity $(\varepsilon)$ = absorptivity $ (\alpha) = 0.21$

At equilibrium, the energy entering the plate ($Q_{\text{enter}}$) equals the energy leaving the plate ($Q_{\text{leave}}$) :

$$Q_{\text{enter}} = Q_{\text{leave}}$$

$$600 = \sigma \varepsilon \left(T_s^4 - T_{\infty}^4 \right)$$

$$600 = 0.21 \times 5.669 \times 10^{-8} \left(T_s^4 - 298^4 \right)$$

Solving for $T_s$ :

$$T_s = 491.33 \, \mathrm{K}$$

Convert to Celsius :

$$T_s = 491.33 \, \mathrm{K} - 273.15 = 218.18^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$

Rounding off to the nearest integer, the radiation equilibrium temperature of the plate is approximately 218°C.

content: For a glass plate transitivity and reflectivity are specified as $$0.86$$ and $$0.08$$ respectively, the absorptivity of the glass plate is

Consider a cylindrical furnace of 5 m diameter and 5 m length with bottom, top and curved surfaces maintained at uniform temperatures of $800 \mathrm{~K}, 1500 \mathrm{~K}$ and 500 K , respectively. The view factor between the bottom and top surfaces, $F_{12}$ is 0.2 . The magnitude of net radiation heat transfer rate between the bottom surface and the curved surface is _________ kW (rounded off to 1 decimal place).

All surfaces of the furnace can be assumed as black.

The Stefan-Boltzmann constant, $\sigma=5.67 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~W} \mathrm{~m}^{-2} \mathrm{~K}^{-4}$.
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} \mathrm{D}=\mathrm{H}=5 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{~F}_{13}= & 0.8, \mathrm{~F}_{23}=0.8, \mathrm{~F}_{13}+\mathrm{F}_{11}+\mathrm{F}_{12}=1 \\ & \mathrm{~F}_{12}=1-\mathrm{F}_{13}=1-0.8=0.2 \\ & \mathrm{~A}_1=\mathrm{A}_2=\frac{\pi}{4} D^2 \\ & \mathrm{~A}_3=\pi \mathrm{DH} \\ & \mathrm{R}_{12}=\frac{1}{A_1 F_{12}}=\frac{1}{\frac{\pi D^2}{4} \times 0.2}=\frac{20}{\pi D^2} \\ & \mathrm{R}_{23}=\frac{1}{A_2 F_{23}}=\frac{1}{\frac{\pi D^2}{4} \times 0.8}=\frac{5}{\pi D^2} \\ & \mathrm{R}_{13}=\frac{1}{A_1 F_{13}}=\frac{1}{\frac{\pi D^2}{4} \times 0.8}=\frac{5}{\pi D^2} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} q_{23} & =\frac{E_{b 2}-E_{b 3}}{R_{23}}=\frac{\sigma\left(T_2^4-T_3^4\right)}{\frac{5}{\pi D^2}} \\ & =5.67 \times 10^{-8}\left(1500^4-500^4\right) \times \frac{\pi(5)^2}{5} \\ & =4453207.586 \mathrm{~W} \\ & =4453.207 \mathrm{~kW} \\ q_{13} & =\frac{E_{b 1}-E_{b 3}}{R_{13}}=\frac{\sigma\left(T_1^4-T_3^4\right)}{R_{13}}=\frac{5.67 \times 10^{-8}\left(800^4-500^4\right)}{\frac{5}{\pi D^2}} \\ & =\frac{5.67 \times 10^{-8} \times \pi(5)^2\left(800^4-500^4\right)}{5} \\ q_{13} & =309141.6707 \mathrm{~W}=309.141 \mathrm{~kW} \end{aligned} $$

content:
Consider a hemispherical furnace of diameter $ D = 6 \text{ m} $ with a flat base. The dome of the furnace has an emissivity of 0.7 and the flat base is a blackbody. The base and the dome are maintained at uniform temperature of 300 K and 1200 K, respectively. Under steady state conditions, the rate of radiation heat transfer from the dome to the base is _______ kW (rounded off to the nearest integer).

Use Stefan-Boltzmann constant = $5.67 \times 10^{-8} \text{ W/(m}^2 \text{ K}^4 \text{)}$

A cylindrical rod of length ℎ and diameter 𝑑 is placed inside a cubic enclosure of side length 𝐿. 𝑆 denotes the inner surface of the cube.

The view-factor FS-S is
explanation:
Given : Inner surface of cube = S

Let, surface of cylinder is 1 and surface of cube is 2 . As we know,

$$ \begin{aligned} & F_{21}+F_{22}=1 \\\\ & F_{22}=F_{s s}=1-F_{21} \\\\ & F_{11}+F_{12}=1 \end{aligned} $$

From diagram : $F_{11}=0$ and $F_{12}=1$

By Reciprocity theorem, $A_1 F_{12}=A_2 F_{21}$

$$ F_{21}=\frac{A_1}{A_2} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & F_{21}=\frac{2 \times \frac{\pi}{4} d^2+\pi d H}{6 L^2}=\frac{\frac{\pi}{2} d^2+\pi d H}{6 L^2} \\\\ & F_{22}=F_{s s}=1-\frac{\pi d h+\frac{\pi}{2} d^2}{6 L^2} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (D).

Two black plates, each one meter square, are placed parallel to each other in such a way that the radiation shape factor for the system is $$0.4.$$ If the plates are maintained at $${800^ \circ }C$$ and $${400^ \circ }C$$ respectively, determine the net radiant heat transfer between the plates. Also calculate the net heat exchange if the plates were infinite in size. Stephen Boltzmann constant $$ = 5.67 \times {10^{ - 8}}\,\,W/{m^2}{K^4}$$
answer:
Net radiant heat transfer $$ = 25.4\,\,kW/{m^2}$$
Net heat exchange $$=63.5$$ $$KW$$
